easy.title: Hello, Environment
easy.description: Write a python script that prints your name, the date, and the python version.
easy.starterCode:
import sys
from datetime import date

name = "Your Name"
today = date.today()
version = sys.version
print(f"Name: {name}")
print(f"Date: {today}")
print(f"Version: {version}")
easy.solution:
import sys
from datetime import date
name = "Your Name"
today = date.today()
v = sys.version
print(f"Name: {name}")
print(f"Date: {today}")
print(f"Version: {v}")
hints: Look up how to get the current date
Find the module that gives version info

medium.title: Environment Inspector
medium.description: List all environment variables, the working directory, and the python executable path.
medium.starterCode:
import os
import sys

for key, value in os.environ.items():
    print(f"{key}={value}")
print(f"\nCWD: {os.getcwd()}")
print(f"EXE: {sys.executable}")
medium.solution:
import os, sys
for k, v in os.environ.items():
    print(f"{k}={v}")
print(f"\nCWD: {os.getcwd()}")
print(f"EXE: {sys.executable}")
hints: Environment variables are stored in a dictionary-like object
The current directory may differ from the script location

hard.title: Mini REPL
hard.description: Build a minimal interactive REPL with error handling.
hints: Use eval() to execute code strings
The readline module helps create interactive programs

debugging.title: Debug the Python Setup
debugging.description: The following python code has setup-related bugs. Find and fix them.
debugging.buggyCode:
import os, sys, datetime
print(os.getcwd)
print("Version: " + sys.version_info)
current_time = datetime.datetime.now
print("Time: " + current_time)
debugging.fixedCode:
import os, sys, datetime
print(os.getcwd())
print(f"Version: {sys.version_info.major}.{sys.version_info.minor}")
now = datetime.datetime.now()
print(f"Time: {now}")
